title: Switched to ubuntu 18.04 (EOL and to old golang)
body:
Beside 17.10 is EOL.
The golang version shipped with 17.10 wont compile:

~~~
vendor/golang.org/x/net/http2/server.go:220: s.RegisterOnShutdown undefined (type *http.Server has no field or method RegisterOnShutdown)
~~~

Fixed when using ubuntu: 18.04

comments.body:
@erkules, thanks for the PR. Could you sign your commit (e.g., `git commit --amend -s`) and then re-push? The change itself LGTM.

@runcom @rhatdan PTAL
@erkules Could you merge your three PR's into one and force push.
I verified this PR, or ubuntu 18.10, was able to fix the issue #587
@erkules, would you rebase the commit? Sorry for the late reply. This PR slipped through.

@erkules, these two commands you'll get just one patch with `Signed-off-by` on top of master:
```
git pull --rebase origin master
git commit --amend --no-edit -s
```

then you can `git push -f` the result
If this passes tests will merge.
LGTM, though a rebase to get rid of 6 extra commits, and notably the old one without a `Signed-Off-By`, would be nice.

Dockerfile.build is now at FROM ubuntu:18.10
So closing.
